DSDS 0.1
Overview Root Schema
example extensions link metadata rich-text status usecase
component pattern style theme token
accessibility anatomy api best-practice design-specifications guideline interaction principle purpose scale state variant

DSDS Guideline — Scoped Unions

Defines scoped guideline unions for each artifact type. Every piece of structured documentation attached to an artifact — best practices, anatomy, API specifications, variant definitions, state descriptions, accessibility specs, purpose, examples, design specifications, principles, scales, and interactions — is a guideline object with a `type` discriminator. Each artifact type accepts only the guideline types relevant to it: component guidelines accept component-specific and general types, style guidelines accept style-specific and general types, etc. This scoping ensures that authors cannot attach irrelevant documentation to an artifact (e.g., an interaction flow on a token, or an API specification on a style). Internal artifact relationships are now expressed through the unified `links` array on each entity rather than through a dedicated guideline type.

Source: guidelines/guideline.schema.json

5 definitions in this file:

  • generalGuideline
  • componentGuideline
  • styleGuideline
  • patternGuideline
  • tokenGuideline

generalGuideline

A guideline type available on all artifact types. General guidelines cover cross-cutting documentation concerns that apply regardless of whether the artifact is a component, token, style, or pattern.

Accepts one of:

  • bestPractices
  • purpose
  • accessibility
  • examples

References: bestPractices, purpose, accessibility, examples

componentGuideline

A guideline type accepted on component artifacts. Includes component-specific types (anatomy, API, variants, states, design specifications) and all general types (best practices, purpose, accessibility, examples).

Accepts one of:

  • anatomy
  • api
  • variants
  • states
  • designSpecifications
  • bestPractices
  • purpose
  • accessibility
  • examples

References: anatomy, api, variants, states, designSpecifications, bestPractices, purpose, accessibility, examples

styleGuideline

A guideline type accepted on style artifacts. Includes style-specific types (principles, scales) and all general types (best practices, purpose, accessibility, examples).

Accepts one of:

  • principles
  • scale
  • bestPractices
  • purpose
  • accessibility
  • examples

References: principles, scale, bestPractices, purpose, accessibility, examples

patternGuideline

A guideline type accepted on pattern artifacts. Includes pattern-specific types (interactions) and all general types (best practices, purpose, accessibility, examples).

Accepts one of:

  • interactions
  • bestPractices
  • purpose
  • accessibility
  • examples

References: interactions, bestPractices, purpose, accessibility, examples

tokenGuideline

A guideline type accepted on token and token group artifacts. Tokens use only general guideline types — they do not have component-specific, style-specific, or pattern-specific documentation concerns.

Accepts one of:

  • bestPractices
  • purpose
  • accessibility
  • examples

References: bestPractices, purpose, accessibility, examples
